title: histiopteris reniformis
intro: Histiopteris reniformis, also known as the Wheki or Maori fern, is a native New Zealand plant species belonging to the family Dicksoniaceae. This plant has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Histiopteris reniformis include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Historically, the plant has been used in herbal preparations like infusions, decoctions, and poultices to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
